questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Two resistors R1 and R2 arranged in parallel combination in an electrical closed circuit are made of the same material and of the same thickness. If the length of R2 is twice the length of R1, then the total resistance R satisfies

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: An electric bulb is rated as 220 V and 80 W. When it is operated on 110 V, the power rating would be:
questions.solution:
To determine the power rating of the electric bulb when operated at a different voltage, let's start by using the given ratings. The bulb is rated as 220 V and 80 W. First, we can calculate the resistance of the bulb using the power formula:

$$ P = \frac{V^2}{R} $$

Given:

$$ P = 80 \, \text{W} $$

$$ V = 220 \, \text{V} $$

Rearranging the formula to solve for resistance \( R \):

$$ R = \frac{V^2}{P} $$

Substituting the values:

$$ R = \frac{220^2}{80} $$

Calculating the resistance:

$$ R = \frac{48400}{80} = 605 \, \Omega $$

Now, we will find the power consumed when the bulb is operated at 110 V. Using the power formula again:

$$ P' = \frac{V'^2}{R} $$

Where:

$$ V' = 110 \, \text{V} $$

$$ R = 605 \, \Omega $$

Substituting these values into the formula:

$$ P' = \frac{110^2}{605} $$

Calculating the power:

$$ P' = \frac{12100}{605} \approx 20 \, \text{W} $$

So, when the bulb is operated at 110 V, the power rating would be approximately 20 W. The correct answer is:

Option D: 20 W

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements is NOT correct?
options: An ammeter is always connected in series in the circuit to measure the current
A Voltmeter is always connected to parallel in a circuit to measure the voltage
A voltmeter has a high resistance and an ammeter has a low resistance
A voltmeter has a low resistance and an ammeter has a high resistance
questions.solution:
The incorrect statement is Option 4.

Explanation:

An ammeter must have a low internal resistance and be connected in series so it doesn’t appreciably change the circuit current.

A voltmeter must have a high internal resistance and be connected in parallel so it doesn’t appreciably draw current.

Option 4 reverses these resistances and is therefore not correct.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: At uniform speed the acceleration is
questions.solution:
Option 3: Zero

Since acceleration is the rate of change of velocity, for uniform speed (and straight-line motion) we have

$$a = \frac{dv}{dt} = 0\,. $$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one among the following diagrams may correctly represent the motion of a skydiver during a jump ?
questions.solution:
CONCEPT:

How a Skydiver Falls

Step 1: Skydiver Jumps

When a skydiver jumps out, gravity pulls them down. Their speed goes up fast at first because they are falling freely through the air.

Step 2: Reaching Terminal Velocity

As the skydiver falls faster, air pushes back harder. Eventually, the push from the air (air resistance) matches the pull of gravity, so the skydiver's speed stops increasing. This steady speed is called terminal velocity.

Step 3: Opening the Parachute

When the parachute opens, there is a sudden big increase in air resistance. This makes the skydiver slow down very quickly.

Speed-Time Graph:

The speed-time graph starts by going up quickly (as the skydiver speeds up). Then, it flattens out (terminal velocity). After the parachute opens, the line drops sharply as the skydiver slows down fast.

EXPLANATION:

Option 1:

This graph is correct. It shows speed going up fast, staying the same for a while (terminal velocity), and then dropping quickly after the parachute opens.

Option 2:

This option is incorrect because it makes the speed go to zero too fast, which doesn't happen in real skydiving.

Option 3:

This option is wrong because it shows speed going up and down in a straight line, as if the skydiver stops suddenly without a parachute. This is unrealistic.

Option 4:

This option almost matches what really happens, but it does not clearly show a flat part for terminal velocity and the slowing down isn't sudden enough when the parachute opens.

So, Option 1 is correct.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements about Light year:

1. Light year is a unit for measurement of very large distances.

2. Light year is a unit for measurement of very large time intervals.

3. Light year is a unit for measurement of intensity of light.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Only statement 1 is correct.

A light year is a unit of distance, not time or intensity of light. It is defined as the distance that light travels in a vacuum in one year. The speed of light is approximately $299,792,458$ meters per second, so over the course of a year, this distance is about:

$$ 1 \text{ light year} = 9.461 \times 10^{15} \text{ meters} $$

Thus, the correct option is D: 1 only.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: In which one among the following situations, the bulb ⊗ would glow the most ? (Consider all batteries are the same)
questions.solution:
CONCEPT:

Power in a Bulb and Types of Circuits

Power, which makes a bulb glow, is found with the formula:

P = V2 / R

Here, P is power, V is voltage, and R is resistance.

In a series circuit, the battery's voltage is split between all the parts. In a parallel circuit, each part gets the full battery voltage.

EXPLANATION:

The bulb shines the brightest when it gets as much voltage as possible because more voltage means more power (according to the formula above).

If the bulb is in a parallel circuit, it will get the full battery voltage. This makes it glow more.

If the bulb is in a series circuit with other things, the voltage is shared, so the bulb gets less voltage and glows less.

So, the bulb will glow the most if it is connected in parallel.

questions.questionText: The time period of a 1 m long pendulum approximates to

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is NOT a basic property of electric charge?
options: Charge on a body is always an integral multiple of an electron or a proton charge
Charges can be created and destroyed in an isolated system
questions.solution:
The statement that is NOT a basic property of electric charge is

Option 4: “Charges can be created and destroyed in an isolated system.”

Here’s why the others are basic properties:

Charges can be added: electric charges obey superposition, so net charge is the algebraic sum of individual charges.

Charge is conserved: in any process, total charge remains constant.

Charge quantization: any observable charge is an integer multiple of the elementary charge $e$.

By contrast, creation or destruction of net charge in isolation would violate charge conservation.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText:
Inhabitants are unaware of the speed of rotation of the planet Earth because

1. the angular velocity is constant for each place on the Earth's surface

2. the atmosphere rotates with the Earth

3. there are no nearby objects, either stationary or moving at a rate different from that of the Earth

Which of the above is/are the correct explanation(s)?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following astronomers proved that the Earth and other planets revolve around the Sun?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

Option A: Copernicus

Nicolaus Copernicus was the astronomer who formulated a model of the universe that placed the Sun rather than the Earth at its center, a concept known as heliocentrism. This model was revolutionary and paved the way for future astronomers to further develop and confirm the idea that the Earth and other planets revolve around the Sun.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: All objects experience a buoyancy when they are immersed in a fluid. Buoyancy is

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText:
When a solid body is partially or completely immersed in a fluid, the fluid exerts an upward force on the body. The magnitude of the force is equal to
1.the mass of the body
2.the weight of the displaced fluid by the body
Which of the above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
The correct choice is 2 only.

By Archimedes’ principle, the buoyant force equals the weight of the fluid displaced:

$$F_{\rm b} = \rho_{\rm fluid}\,V_{\rm disp}\,g$$

where

• ρ₍fluid₎ is the fluid’s density

• V₍disp₎ is the volume of fluid displaced

• g is the acceleration due to gravity.

It is not equal to the mass of the body.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: If three resistors of 1 Ohm each connect in parallel to each other the resultant resistance is
questions.solution:
For resistors in parallel, the reciprocal of the total resistance is the sum of reciprocals:

$$ \frac1{R_{\rm eq}} = \frac1{R_1} + \frac1{R_2} + \frac1{R_3} = \frac1{1} + \frac1{1} + \frac1{1} = 3 $$

So

$$ R_{\rm eq} = \frac1{3}\,\Omega. $$

That corresponds to Option 2: 1/3 Ohm.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Two conducting wires of the same material and of equal lengths and equal diameters are first connected in parallel and then in series in a circuit across the same potential difference. The ratio of heat produced in parallel and series combinations is
questions.solution:
Let the resistance of each wire be $R$.

Since both wires are identical:

  • In series, equivalent resistance:

$$ R_s = R + R = 2R $$

  • In parallel, equivalent resistance:

$$ R_p = \frac{R \cdot R}{R + R} = \frac{R}{2} $$

Now, heat produced in a circuit is given by Joule’s law:

$$ H = \frac{V^2 t}{R} $$

(where $V$ and $t$ are same in both cases)

So,

For parallel combination:

$$ H_p = \frac{V^2 t}{R_p} = \frac{V^2 t}{R/2} = \frac{2V^2 t}{R} $$

For series combination:

$$ H_s = \frac{V^2 t}{R_s} = \frac{V^2 t}{2R} $$

Now, ratio:

$$ \frac{H_p}{H_s} = \frac{2V^2 t / R}{V^2 t / (2R)} = 4 $$

So, the ratio is:

$$ 4:1 $$

Correct option: Option 2
